denial_pattern_sources: contraindication_to_step_therapy: AMA 2023 Prior Authorization Physician Survey; State step-therapy exception laws (NY, TX, IL)
peer_to_peer_window_verbal_only: AMA 2023 Prior Authorization Physician Survey; documented in multiple state PA reform laws (TX HB 2002, VA SB 1272)
denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

We received your provider's request on 03/21/2026 for Transcatheter aortic valve replacement (TAVR) evaluation related to Symptomatic aortic stenosis, moderate (I35.0). After review, we cannot approve this request.

Our review applied UnitedHealthcare Medical Management Guidelines and Optum clinical criteria. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Transcatheter aortic valve replacement (TAVR) evaluation with an out-of-network provider is not covered without a gap exception. In-network alternatives are available.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with out of network no authorization. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the verbal timeframe communicated to office staff (not confirmed in this letter).

You have the right to appeal this determination. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this letter. Include your member identification number and the service reference shown on your explanation of benefits.

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management
clinical_context: The 65-year-old member has Symptomatic aortic stenosis, moderate (I35.0). She has been treated under commercial coverage with Transcatheter aortic valve replacement (TAVR) evaluation recommended as the next appropriate step. Syncope episode 2025; valve area 1.0 cm² with mean gradient 38 mmHg. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that out of network no authorization bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.
